spec_match.rationale: Demonstrates the exact MIME-filtering pattern the spec encourages: read `dataTransfer.items[0].type` inside `beforeinput`, compare against an allow-list, and call `event.preventDefault()` on miss.

interactive.rationale: MIME toggles drive a live allow/deny policy, simulator buttons dispatch synthetic `beforeinput` events with DataTransfer files, and the contenteditable target handles real Android IME insertions when available.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Blurb promises a toggle-driven gating demo with logged accept/reject decisions; the page delivers that.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers explicit MIME allow-listing, image/* wildcard acceptance, empty-type fallback, multi-file packs, and a deny-list branch for image/svg+xml.

references.rationale: The see-also section links directly to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5484282563919872.

design_system.rationale: Variables are used for local colors, button background overrides pair with color, and accept/reject/info log markers are inline SVG using currentColor.

summary: The filter now handles real and simulated beforeinput media insertions with explicit MIME toggles, wildcard and empty-type branches, SVG deny-listing, multi-file packs, inline SVG log markers, and a ChromeStatus reference.
